_meta.note: Single source of truth for AnthonyCharts. Edit this file, run build/build.js, verify with build/verify.js. Drawers/ticker/tiles/arcs are generated into public/index.html.

Prior weights renormalized x0.9 (IFCN 50->45, RSF 30->27, NewsGuard 20->18). Replace placeholder component subscores with real IFCN/RSF/NewsGuard values before enabling.

edge.desc: Composite of GPR Index, active conflicts, public stress, wealth inequality, species loss, and expert forecasts.

good.desc: Tracks humanitarian aid, scientific breakthroughs, poverty reduction, conservation wins, social mobility, and global cooperation.

ticker: ❄️ ARCTIC ICE: Record-low March extent · NSIDC
☄️ NEO WATCH: 4 close approaches this week · No threats
☀️ SOLAR ACTIVITY: MODERATE — G1 Storm Watch

edge.title: World On Edge Index

rows.note: US–Iran war · Hormuz closed · index record

levels.label: Very High — systemic risk indicators active

edge.body: The World On Edge Index holds at 88/100 — one point off its March record. Day 103 of the US–Iran war brought the sharpest oscillation yet: the ceasefire broke open June 7–8 into the worst Israel–Iran exchange in months, US strikes resumed, and Iran hit US facilities in Bahrain, Jordan, and Kuwait. Yet the economic axis is easing — Hormuz ship traffic is increasing per the US Energy Secretary, Brent has cooled to the low $90s from a $138 wartime peak, and a US ceasefire framework is on the table against hawkish resistance. Kinetic risk and trade stress are now pointing in opposite directions for the first time since February. Prior context:  GPR hit 127.89 in January 2026, driven by Eurasia conflict language in major global newspapers. ACLED track...
edge.method: Five inputs normalized 0–100, weighted and summed. GPR (35%): Caldara & Iacoviello index vs 0–200 range. ACLED (25%): active conflict zones vs 0–80 range. Gallup (15%): worry index as published. Conflict Deaths (15%): YTD deaths vs 50,000 threshold. Species Threat (10%): threatened % vs 50% threshold. Weights at anthonycharts.com/composites-v1.0.json
edge.share: World On Edge: 88/100 — Very High. US–Iran war Day 103: worst strikes in months, yet Hormuz traffic rising and Brent down to ~$93. Calm Window 31%. anthonycharts.com 🌍

good.title: World Goodness Index

rows.note: Current — war diverting aid and cooperation

good.body: At 47/100 the World Goodness Index holds below its pre-COVID baseline of ~61. Aid volume and poverty reduction are the strongest inputs. Conservati...
good.method: Six inputs normalized 0–100, weighted and summed. Aid (20%): OCHA commitments vs 30B USD. Poverty (20%): World Bank rate inverted. Breakthroughs (15%): OWID annual count vs 50. Treaties (15%): UN quarterly signatures vs 30. Social Mobility (15%): World Bank GSMI 0–100. Conservation (15%): % of 30x30 target achieved. Weights at anthonycharts.com/composites-v1.0.json
good.share: World Goodness: 47/100 — below historical average. Aid holding. Inequality dragging. Not the inverse of Edge — independently constructed. anthonycharts.com 💚

temp.title: Global Temp Anomaly

levels.label: Paris Agreement threshold — first sustained breach projected 2030–35

temp.body: We are 1.42°C above the 1850–1900 pre-industrial average (12-month trailing, Copernicus ERA5 through May 2026). May 2026 was the joint second-warmest May on record, sea surface temperatures hit their second-highest level, and El Niño conditions are developing — forecasters warn 2027 could push to new records. 2024 broke all records at +1.62°C — the first calendar year above 1.5...
temp.method: 12-month trailing average of global surface temperature anomaly above 1850–1900 pre-industrial mean. Primary: Copernicus C3S ERA5 reanalysis. Cross-referenced with NOAA GISSTEMP and Berkeley Earth. Arc = position in 0–2°C Paris risk corridor. Updated monthly.
temp.share: Global temp: +1.42°C above pre-industrial (12-mo avg). 2024 hit +1.62°C — hottest year on record. Paris threshold: 1.5°C. anthonycharts.com 🌡️

sea.title: Sea Level Rise

trend.label: Rising and accelerating — rate 2.5x higher than 1990s

levels.label: High-risk — major coastal infrastructure at risk

sea.body: Global mean sea level up 112mm since 1993. Current rate of +4.1mm/year has accelerated 2.5x from the 1990s (+1.7mm/yr). Primary drivers: thermal ex...
sea.method: Continuous satellite altimetry: TOPEX/Poseidon (1993) → Jason-1/2/3 → Sentinel-6 Michael Freilich (2020–present). Monthly global mean averages from NASA JPL Physical Oceanography DAAC. Arc = position in 0–200mm scale. Rate acceleration from linear trend analysis of 5-year segments.
sea.share: Sea level: +112mm since 1993. Rate +4.1mm/yr — 2.5x faster than the 1990s. Greenland driving acceleration. Full trend: anthonycharts.com 🌊

arctic.title: Arctic Sea Ice Extent

trend.label: Below seasonal median — long-term declining trend

rows.note: Lowest March extent on record — C3S

levels.label: Climatological baseline for this calendar date

arctic.body: March 2026 set the lowest Arctic sea ice extent on record for the month (Copernicus C3S) — the winter maximum ended at a record deficit, and the melt season is now underway from that depressed starting point. February stood 1.2M km² below the 1981–2010 median. Seasonal variation is completely no...
arctic.method: NSIDC Sea Ice Index via satellite passive microwave (SMMR, SSM/I, SSMIS). Extent = total ocean area with at least 15% ice concentration. Updated daily. Compared against 1981–2010 median for the same calendar day to remove seasonal signal. Arc = percentile within seasonal historical range.
arctic.share: Arctic ice: 13.8M km² — 1.2M km² below seasonal median. Long-term decline continues. February 2024 was record low winter month. anthonycharts.com ❄️

co2.title: CO₂ Concentration

trend.label: Rising — fastest rate in 800,000-year ice core record

levels.label: Safe zone upper bound — Hansen, 1988 (now exceeded)
High-risk — 2°C warming likely at sustained levels

co2.body: CO₂ at 431.1 ppm (April 2026, NOAA Mauna Loa) — 54% above pre-industrial. Year-over-year growth slowed to +1.5 ppm as La Niña damped the increment, but the long-run climb is the fastest in the 800,000-year ice core record, and forecasters expect the developing El Niño to re-accelerate it. The Kee...
co2.method: Continuous measurement at NOAA Mauna Loa Observatory since 1958 (the Keeling Curve). Weekly averages from NOAA Global Monitoring Laboratory. Cross-referenced with Scripps Institution CO₂ program. Arc = position between 280ppm (pre-industrial) and 500ppm (extreme risk ceiling).
co2.share: CO₂: 431.1 ppm — 54% above pre-industrial. Rising faster than any point in 800,000 years. The Keeling Curve climbs. anthonycharts.com 🌿

planet.title: Exoplanet Discovery

trend.label: Rising — JWST accelerating discovery and atmosphere analysis

levels.label: Current count — confirmed via peer review (Feb 2026)
No confirmed biosignatures yet — JWST now capable of detection

planet.body: 6,128 confirmed planets orbiting other stars — a number that was zero 34 years ago. NASA announced the 6,000 milestone on September 17, 2025, and the Nancy Grace Roman Space Telescope launches August 30, 2026 — projected to discover 100,000+ more. 183 sit in their star's habitable zone where liquid water could exist. JWST can now analyze exoplanet atmospheres directly for chemical biosignatures. No conclusive biological signals detected yet, but the search has genuinely begun.
planet.method: Raw count from NASA Exoplanet Archive. Confirmed status requires: peer-reviewed publication, independent verification via secondary detection method, and archive acceptance. Habitable zone defined by orbital distance allowing liquid surface water. Arc = pace toward 10,000 milestone by 2030.
planet.share: 6,128 confirmed exoplanets — past the 6,000 milestone. Roman Telescope launches Aug 2026. JWST now reading atmospheres. The universe is full of worlds. anthonycharts.com 🪐

alien.title: Technosignature Watch

trend.label: Stable — 3 active candidates, none confirmed, none new

levels.label: Current — no confirmed extraterrestrial signal ever
Passes RFI filter + multi-beam — still unresolved

alien.body: Three signals remain in active review as technosignature candidates. The track record: every prior candidate has resolved to natural causes or radi...
alien.method: Manual count from Breakthrough Listen published candidate reports and SETI Institute announcements. Candidate criteria: passed RFI filtering, observed in multiple independent beams, no confirmed natural explanation at time of logging. Updated as reported in peer-reviewed publications.
alien.share: 3 technosignature candidates active. 0 confirmed ever. Wow! Signal still unexplained after 49 years. Breakthrough Listen: 1M+ stars scanned. anthonycharts.com 👽

solar.title: Solar Activity

trend.label: Declining — past Solar Cycle 25 maximum, activity trending lower

levels.label: Current — weak grid fluctuations at high latitudes
Power grid fluctuations, satellite drag increases
Grid collapse risk, widespread satellite damage

solar.body: Solar Cycle 25 peaked in late 2025 at higher intensity than NOAA predictions — a notable forecasting miss. G1 is the mildest storm classification: ...
solar.method: 24-hour peak geomagnetic storm rating from NOAA Space Weather Prediction Center (SWPC). G-scale from Kp-index: G1=Kp≥5, G2=Kp≥6, G3=Kp≥7, G4=Kp≥8, G5=Kp=9. Sunspot number from WDC-SILSO, Royal Observatory of Belgium — international standard since 1849.
solar.share: Solar Activity: G1 today. Cycle 25 peaked higher than predicted. Now descending. NOAA tracks daily. anthonycharts.com ☀️

neo.title: Near-Earth Object Watch

trend.label: Catalog growing — more known = safer, not more dangerous

levels.label: Current — no objects on impact trajectory
Within 0.05 AU (~7.5M km) — tracked, not threatening
>140m diameter + passes within 0.05 AU of Earth
Would trigger active planetary defense response

neo.body: NASA tracks 36,000+ near-Earth objects. This week: 4 close approaches, all at safe distances measured in lunar distances (LD). None classified as i...
neo.method: NASA CNEOS close approach database. Close approach = within 0.05 AU (~7.5M km). Impact probability from JPL Sentry automated orbital mechanics. Potentially hazardous: >140m + intersects within 0.05 AU. Updated daily from automated telescope observations worldwide.
neo.share: 4 NEO close approaches this week — all safe. 36,000+ known, 0 threats. DART proved we can deflect them. anthonycharts.com ☄️

gini.title: Wealth Inequality

trend.label: Widening — COVID accelerated gap, no recovery

gini.body: Global wealth Gini of 0.67: the top 1% own 38% of all global wealth; the bottom 50% share 2%. Inequality widened sharply during COVID — asset price...
gini.method: Global Gini from World Inequality Database (WID.world) — maintained by Piketty, Chancel, Saez, and Zucman. Gini: 0 = perfect equality, 1 = perfect inequality. Arc normalized against 0–1.0 scale, with 0.8 as practical ceiling. Updated annually with World Inequality Report.
gini.share: Global Gini: 0.67. Top 1% own 38% of wealth. Bottom 50% share 2%. COVID widened the gap. No recovery yet. anthonycharts.com ⚖️

mobility.title: Social Mobility Index

trend.label: Stalled — no meaningful global progress since 2020

levels.label: Below average — South Asia, Sub-Saharan Africa

mobility.body: The World Bank Global Social Mobility Index scores countries 0–100 across 10 pillars. The global average of 53.4 reflects a world where birthplace ...
mobility.method: World Bank GSMI: 10 equal-weight pillars — health, education access, education quality, lifelong learning, technology access, work opportunities, fair wages, working conditions, social protection, institutions. Supplemented by Opportunity Insights intergenerational income mobility data. Annual publication.
mobility.share: Social Mobility: 53.4/100 globally. Denmark leads at 83.6. US: 76.1. Progress stalled since 2020. Where you're born still largely determines outcomes. anthonycharts.com 📈

conflict.title: Conflict Deaths

trend.label: Elevated — above pre-2022 baseline, Ukraine and Gaza primary

levels.label: Current pace — elevated above pre-2022 avg
Very High — major interstate war level

conflict.body: 28,400 battle deaths recorded YTD in 2026 across 56 active conflict zones. Ukraine and Gaza account for the majority. 2022 Ukraine invasion drove t...
conflict.method: Primary: ACLED — real-time coding of political violence events from media, NGOs, and government sources across 200+ countries. Cross-referenced with UCDP (Uppsala Conflict Data Program) for battle death estimates. Battle deaths only — excludes indirect deaths from displacement or disease. Updated monthly.
conflict.share: 28,400 conflict deaths YTD in 2026 — 56 active zones. Ukraine and Gaza primary. Above pre-2022 baseline. Paired with Peacekeeping Reach at anthonycharts.com 💔

peace.title: Peacekeeping Reach

trend.label: Declining — 27% below 2015 peak, funding under pressure

peace.body: 87,000 UN peacekeeping personnel deployed across 12 missions — 27% below the 2015 peak of 120,000. DR Congo (MONUSCO) remains the largest. The $6.4B peacekeeping budget is less than 0.5% of global military spending — the world's response capacity is proportional to its crises.
peace.method: UN DPPA monthly fact sheets. Personnel = troops + police + civilian international/national staff. Budget from UN General Assembly appropriations. Mission count = active mandated operations. Updated monthly from official UN reporting.
peace.share: 87,000 peacekeepers, 12 missions — 27% below 2015 peak. Budget $6.4B = <0.5% of global military spend. 56 conflict zones vs 12 missions. anthonycharts.com 🕊️

species.title: Species Threat Index

trend.label: Rising — threat rate accelerating, 902 confirmed extinct since 1500

levels.label: Elevated crisis — mass extinction indicators active

species.body: 44,016 species classified as threatened (Vulnerable, Endangered, or Critically Endangered) — 28% of all assessed. 902 confirmed extinctions since 1...
species.method: IUCN Red List — the world's most comprehensive biodiversity database. Assessed across nine categories from Least Concern to Extinct. Threatened = Vulnerable + Endangered + Critically Endangered. Updated quarterly. Arc = threatened % of assessed, normalized against 50% ceiling.
species.share: 44,016 species threatened — 28% of all assessed. 902 extinct since 1500. Paired with Conservation Coverage. anthonycharts.com 🦏

conserve.title: Conservation Coverage

trend.label: Growing — but below pace required for 30x30 by 2030

conserve.body: 17.6% of land and 8.2% of oceans are formally protected — up from 14% land and 3% ocean in 2010. The 30x30 target was agreed at COP15 in Montreal i...
conserve.method: Protected Planet database by UNEP-WCMC and IUCN. Counts nationally designated protected areas and OECMs (Other Effective Area-Based Conservation Measures). Updated annually with country submissions to the Convention on Biological Diversity. Arc = % of 30x30 land target achieved.
conserve.share: 17.6% land protected. 8.2% ocean. Racing toward 30x30 by 2030 — ocean will fall short at current pace. Paired with Species Threat. anthonycharts.com 🌿

supply.title: Global Trade Stress

trend.label: Easing — down from the 88 peak, still elevated

levels.label: High stress — visible supply chain disruption
Current (81) — crisis-level, easing from the 88 peak

supply.body: Global Trade Stress at 81/100 — high and easing from the 88 wartime peak. Houthi attacks on Red Sea shipping are rerouting cargo arou...
supply.method: Experimental composite: Freightos Baltic Index (35%) — global container freight benchmark normalized historically. Manufacturing PMI inverted (35%) — S&P Global composite, inverted so contraction = higher stress. World Bank trade volume momentum (30%) — month-over-month change. Weights at anthonycharts.com/composites-v1.0.json
supply.share: Trade Stress: 81/100 — high, easing from the 88 peak. Red Sea disruptions, PMI 47.2 (contraction), freight +18% YOY. Full breakdown: anthonycharts.com 🚢

truth.title: Information Integrity Index

trend.label: Declining — down 4 pts YOY, 10+ pts below pre-COVID

levels.label: High integrity — most major claims accurate
Low — majority of claims unverified or false

truth.body: Information Integrity at 49.5%: roughly half of major claims reviewed by IFCN-certified fact-checkers did not meet accuracy standards. Down 4 point...
truth.method: Experimental composite: IFCN claim accuracy rate (50%) — from IFCN-verified fact-checkers internationally. RSF Press Freedom Index inverted (30%) — higher freedom = higher integrity score. NewsGuard credibility average (20%) — ratings of major news and information sources. Experimental — weights subject to revision, published at anthonycharts.com/composites-v1.0.json
truth.share: Information Integrity: 49.5% — roughly half of major claims not meeting accuracy standards. Down 4pts YOY. 10+ pts below pre-COVID. anthonycharts.com 🎯

sources.name: Signal Scanner
sources.desc: Anthony Charts · Headline framing integrity, scored per sweep

signal_trends._note: Guardrail 5: rows below are the prototype samples; the footer shows a SAMPLE DATA label while sample=true. Row contract: {score, headline, source, time, trend, query, match, link, signalUrl} — `link` (source article, new tab) and `signalUrl` (Signal article page, /article/:id on anthony-signal) make the headline and score ring clickable; sample rows carry null for both because their headlines have no real articles. The real pipeline fills them.

rows.headline: NATO convenes emergency consultations after Baltic drone incursions
Freight rates spike as Red Sea rerouting stretches into third month
CO₂ passes 431 ppm as Mauna Loa confirms new seasonal record
TESS data release pushes confirmed exoplanet count past 6,100
